arguments.query: Adding a New API Route create apps/web/src/app/api route.ts add to lib/db/queries Zod tests route.test.ts pnpm lint pnpm type-check pnpm test Route Handler Pattern requireAuth Zod schema delegate to service successResponse handleError

tool_results.content:
{'sections': [{'title': 'Adding a New API Route', 'content': '### Adding a New API Route

1. Create `apps/web/src/app/api/[resource]/route.ts`
2. Add to `lib/db/queries/` for database access
3. Add validation schema using Zod
4. Add tests in `route.test.ts`
5. Run: `pnpm lint && pnpm type-check && pnpm test`
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [344, 350], 'relevance_score': 1.0, 'token_count': 74, 'truncated': False}, {'title': '✅ Test 3: Existing API Keys', 'content': '### ✅ Test 3: Existing API Keys

**Status:** PASSED ✅

**What Was Tested:**

- Tested production API with valid project API key
- Endpoint: `POST https://api.snipara.com/v1/snipara/mcp`

**Results:**

- API responds (returns 500 for different reason - see below)
- Authentication layer accepts valid key
- No regressions in key validation logic

---
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [1454, 1469], 'relevance_score': 1.0, 'token_count': 90, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'API Routes (9 files)', 'content': '### API Routes (9 files)
```
apps/web/src/app/api/agents/
├── route.ts                       # GET overview stats
├── subscription/
│   ├── route.ts                   # GET/POST subscription
│   └── checkout/route.ts          # POST Stripe checkout
├── memory/
│   ├── route.ts                   # GET list, POST create
│   └── [id]/route.ts              # GET, DELETE
├── swarms/
│   ├── route.ts                   # GET list, POST create
│   └── [id]/
│       ├── route.ts               # GET, PATCH, DELETE
│       └── tasks/route.ts         # GET tasks
└── cache/
    └── route.ts                   # GET stats, DELETE invalidate
```
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [46954, 46971], 'relevance_score': 0.9313395171915508, 'token_count': 170, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'API Routes Created', 'content': '### API Routes Created
- `GET/POST/DELETE /api/projects/[projectId]/api-keys` - API key management
- `GET/PUT/DELETE /api/teams/[teamId]` - Team settings
- `GET/POST /api/invites/[token]` - Invite acceptance


### Schema Changes
- Added `notificationPrefs` JSON field to User model
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [15384, 15390], 'relevance_score': 0.9076977313899869, 'token_count': 80, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'Projects Router', 'content': "### Projects Router
```typescript
// server/trpc/procedures/projects.ts

export const projectsRouter = router({
  list: protectedProcedure.query(/* list user's projects */),
  getBySlug: protectedProcedure.input(z.string()).query(/* get project */),
  create: protectedProcedure.input(createProjectSchema).mutation(/* create */),
  update: protectedProcedure.input(updateProjectSchema).mutation(/* update */),
  delete: protectedProcedure.input(z.string()).mutation(/* delete */),
  syncFromGitHub: protectedProcedure.input(z.string()).mutation(/* sync */),
});
```
", 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [12403, 12415], 'relevance_score': 0.8964224181615487, 'token_count': 115, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'Route Handler Pattern', 'content': '### Route Handler Pattern

```typescript
// ✅ CORRECT: Thin route handler
export async function POST(request: Request) {
  try {
    const user = await requireAuth();
    const body = createProjectSchema.parse(await request.json());
    const result = await createProject(body, user.id);
    return successResponse(result);
  } catch (error) {
    return handleError(error);
  }
}
```

**Route handlers must:**

1. Authenticate (requireAuth)
2. Validate input (Zod schema)
3. Delegate to service/query
4. Return response
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [13350, 13371], 'relevance_score': 0.8854887810915478, 'token_count': 119, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'Agents Dashboard (`apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/agents/page.tsx`)', 'content': '#### Agents Dashboard (`apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/agents/page.tsx`)

| Issue                                    | Fix                                               |
| ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| "Popular" badge clipped on pricing cards | Added `overflow-visible` to card, `z-10` to badge |


### Documentation
', 'file': '.claude/commands/debug.md', 'lines': [43913, 43919], 'relevance_score': 0.8748815212475174, 'token_count': 63, 'truncated': False}, {'title': 'Automation API Routes', 'content': '## Automation API Routes

| Route                                                       | Methods | Description                    |
| ----------------------------------------------------------- | ------- | ------------------------------ |
| `/api/projects/:projectIdOrSlug/automation`                 | GET/PUT | Get/update automation settings |
| `/api/projects/:projectIdOrSlug/automation/inject`          | POST    | Inject context (from hooks)    |
| `/api/projects/:projectIdOrSlug/automation/inject`          | GET     | Retrieve stored context        |
| `/api/projects/:projectIdOrSlug/automation/memory-settings` | GET     | Get memory injection settings  |
| `/api/mcp/:projectIdOrSlug`                                 | POST    | MCP tool proxy                 |

**Note:** All automation routes support both database IDs (`cm5xyz...`) and project slugs (`snipara`) as the `:projectIdOrSlug` parameter.

---

response:
Based on the project documentation, follow these standards when adding a new API endpoint:

Required files & location
- Create the route file at: apps/web/src/app/api/[resource]/route.ts
- Put any database access (CRUD) in lib/db/queries/ (routes should not directly implement DB logic)

Route handler pattern (must follow)
- Keep the route handler thin. Example pattern from docs:
  - Authenticate (requireAuth)
  - Validate input with a Zod schema
  - Delegate work to a service or a query function
  - Return a success response
  - Wrap in try/catch and call the project error handler on failure (handleError)
- Explicitly: Authenticate → Validate → Delegate → Return

Validation and tests
- Add a Zod validation schema for the route input
- Add tests (route.test.ts) covering the new route

Build / quality checks
- After adding the route run: pnpm lint && pnpm type-check && pnpm test

Architecture / layering rules
- Respect import/layering hierarchy: Routes (app/api/) → Services (lib/services/) → Repositories / Queries (lib/db/queries/) → Utils & Types
- Lower layers must not import from higher layers; routes delegate to services/queries

TypeScript coding standards to follow
- Project enforces strict TypeScript (strict mode, noImplicitAny, strictNullChecks, noUnusedLocals/Parameters)
- Exported functions must have explicit return types
- Do not use any; use explicit types or unknown with type guards
- Use async/await (avoid raw Promise chaining)
- Follow import ordering groups: external packages → internal packages → absolute imports → relative imports
- Use interface for extendable object shapes and type for unions/intersections

Other explicit guidance from docs
- Keep route handlers thin and delegate business logic
- Add DB access logic to lib/db/queries/ (centralized CRUD only)
- All the above steps for adding a route are listed in “Adding a New API Route” in the coding standards

If you want, I can produce a minimal route skeleton (following the documented pattern) and a checklist you can paste into a PR description.
